page: Amazon Web Services c7i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8488C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features 96 virtual CPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor runs at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz and is supported by a 105 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with the PassMark CPU score and CPU encryption tests reaching top-tier status. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read-only bandwidth for small cached blocks and strong bandwidth for larger uncached blocks. Cryptographic and vector processing capabilities are enhanced by hardware support for AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions. In application-level benchmarks, the instance demonstrates strong performance in Redis SET operations, static web serving, and medium-sized LLM inference tasks, though text generation for large 70B models falls into the average tier.

Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the c7i.24xlarge provides a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. The primary tradeoff of this instance is the complete absence of local NVMe storage, requiring reliance on network-attached storage for persistent data. Given its high compute density, fast DDR5 memory, and advanced instruction sets, this instance is optimized for compute-heavy workloads such as high-performance web serving, cryptographic processing, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 192.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Performance benchmarks show top-tier results in PassMark CPU and encryption tests, alongside strong multi-core execution and memory bandwidth. Hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX further boost vector and matrix calculations. This profile makes the c7i.24xlarge highly efficient for compute-intensive workloads, including static web serving, Redis databases, cryptographic operations, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier CPU and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.24xlarge is an Intel-powered compute-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 38 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-core Intel Xeon compute instance featuring DDR5 memory and advanced matrix extensions for intensive parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8488C at 3.2 GHz
192.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier performance in CPU benchmarks and cryptographic operations
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector math
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for compute-heavy tasks, web serving, and CPU-based inference
